metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge AC3 into AC2 (or drop AC3) since both assert the same local-commit-reachability property; if the gc-prune angle is worth preserving, fold it as a parenthetical in AC2 rather than a separate criterion.
findings.finding: AC3 is a near-duplicate of the local-commit-reachability sub-clause already stated in AC2: AC2 asserts 'the local event commit … [is] reachable from HEAD' and AC3 asserts 'the local commit … remains reachable' with only a gc-prune elaboration added, making it redundant.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to read: 'After `sync.reconverge`, both the local event commit and the remote event commit are reachable from HEAD (i.e. would survive `git gc --prune=now`), and both event dirs exist in the working tree.' Then remove AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Drop the optional clone-helper abstraction: inline the `git clone` subprocess call directly in the test (matching the `test_snapshot_environments.py` pattern), unless at least two other call-sites exist or will exist in the same PR.
findings.finding: The plan floats adding 'a small clone helper' as an optional new abstraction. There is only one existing inline `git clone` call in the test suite (`tests/interfaces/store/test_snapshot_environments.py` uses `subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', ...])` directly), so a named helper function would have at most two call-sites total — below the Rule-of-Three threshold for justified abstraction.
findings.location: Scope section: 'A small clone helper may be added if clearer.'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace any `_clone_tracker(origin, tracker)` helper with an inline call:
```python
subprocess.run(
    ["git", "clone", "-b", "tickets", "-q", str(origin), str(tracker)],
    check=True, capture_output=True,
)
```
This matches the existing `test_snapshot_environments.py` pattern and keeps the test fully self-contained.

findings.finding: The plan claims the new test can be written by 'reusing the file's `_new_tickets_repo`' helper for the tracker setup, but `_new_tickets_repo` only calls `git init` — it cannot create a clone with shared history. Step 1 explicitly requires 'the tracker is cloned from [origin] (so they share history and `refs/heads/tickets` starts at base)', which requires `git clone` (or manual post-init shared-base setup), not `_new_tickets_repo`.
findings.location: ## What, step 1 / ## Scope — 'reusing the file's `_new_tickets_repo` / `_commit_event` / `_git` helpers'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the tracker-init step with:
```python
subprocess.run(['git', 'clone', '-q', str(origin), str(tracker)], check=True)
_git(tracker, 'config', 'user.email', 't@t')
_git(tracker, 'config', 'user.name', 't')
```
This creates a tracker whose `refs/heads/tickets` starts at the same base commit as origin, satisfying the plan's shared-history requirement. `_new_tickets_repo` is still reusable for the origin setup.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
